title: Pocket Balance Pulse
description: Balance a wobbling mobile sculpture by replaying its pulse rhythm before the hanging rig tips and snaps.

howToPlayJson.title: Pocket Balance Pulse
howToPlayJson.objective: Keep a hanging phone-charms mobile steady for a 2-4 minute run by memorizing and replaying ever-longer pulse sequences on the correct dangling weights.

controls.input: Tap the glowing hanging weight in the heard order
controls.outcome: Matches the rhythm, steadies the mobile, and extends the combo

controls.input: Press and hold the center knot with one thumb during replay
controls.outcome: Slows the tilt for 1 second but halves points earned on that beat

controls.input: Tap the flashing arrow between rounds
controls.outcome: Accepts the current wind-shift rule so you can start the next sequence

steps: A short melody plays across 3-5 hanging weights around a central knot, each weight lighting and chiming in order.
The player repeats the sequence by tapping the same weights before the balance meter fully leans left or right.
Each cleared round adds one beat, increases playback speed, and makes the mobile sway harder between taps.
Every few seconds a Wind Shift twist changes the rule: either heavy-side beats must be mirrored to the opposite side, or center beats mustbe
howToPlayJson.scoring: Each correct beat is worth 10 points times the current combo multiplier. The multiplier rises by +1 every 5 perfect beats up to x8. Using Brace balance preserves the run but scores only half for those beats. Missing a be
tips: Listen for pitch as well as position; each weight keeps the same note all run.
Use Brace only when the mobile is near red tilt; overusing it kills high-score pace.
When Wind Shift appears, read the icon first: mirror arrows mean opposite side, pulse ring means repeat center twice.
